title: Residents voice frustrations with snow removal during City Council hearing
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Residents and City Council members expressed frustration over Boston's snow removal efforts after major winter storms in 2026.
Concerns included uncleared sidewalks and bus stops, hindering accessibility for people with disabilities and creating safety issues.
Officials discussed proposals for improving snow removal, including using snow melting machines and establishing a volunteer 'snow corps'.

title: RFK Jr. blasts Dunkin’ as Healey replies, ‘come and take it’
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Health Secretary RFK Jr. criticized Dunkin' and Starbucks over high-sugar drinks at a rally in Austin, Texas.
Gov. Maura Healey responded to RFK Jr.'s comments by sharing a meme supporting Dunkin's iconic status in Massachusetts.
Social media reacted with humor, referencing New England's revolutionary history and emphasizing regional loyalty to Dunkin'.

title: Stats in Southie is closing for now, but has plans to reopen ‘bigger and better’
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Stats Bar & Grille in South Boston is closing at the end of March, with plans to reopen in 2027.
The new Stats 2.0 will be part of a mixed-use development at its current Dorchester Street location.
The redeveloped building will feature 15 residential units and a two-story restaurant housing the new Stats.

title: Dine at the Boston table famous for JFK’s proposal to Jacqueline Bouvier
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Parker's Restaurant at Omni Parker House in Boston is known for JFK's 1953 proposal to Jacqueline Bouvier.
Martin's Tavern in Washington, D.C., also claims JFK proposed to Jackie there, both spots marked by plaques.
Omni Parker House, established in 1855, underwent a $65 million renovation in 2025, drawing visitors for its Kennedy ties.

title: She’s running her fifth Boston Marathon in support of Dana-Farber
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Amanda Bathory, 45, from Auburndale, runs her fifth Boston Marathon supporting Dana-Farber Cancer Institute in 2026.
Bathory runs the marathon to honor her late mother and supports cancer research and patient care initiatives.
She sees the marathon as a personal journey, transforming grief into purpose and supporting those affected by cancer.

title: A rat race to the rescue: MSPCA-Angell seeks homes after 163 rodents surrendered
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MSPCA-Angell seeks homes for 163 rats surrendered from a home in Essex County, its largest such case in five years.
53 rats remain with MSPCA-Angell for adoption, while others were dispersed to shelters in Massachusetts and New Hampshire.
The rat surrender has stretched resources, but MSPCA-Angell emphasizes care and adoption over traditional cats and dogs.

title: ‘Nobody wants to see this’: Knicks fan Rich Eisen’s rant about the Celtics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Rich Eisen, a Knicks fan, expressed frustration at the Celtics' success despite their roster changes and Tatum's injury.
The Celtics recently defeated the Bucks 108-81, showcasing strong performances even without Jaylen Brown or Jayson Tatum.
Eisen humorously lamented that 'nobody wants to see' the Celtics succeed, noting his son's Boston sports fandom.

title: Boston 25 News reporter Drew Karedes leaves after nine years
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Drew Karedes announced his departure from Boston 25 News after nine years via social media.
Karedes began his career 19 years ago in Green Bay, Wisconsin, and joined Boston 25 in March 2017.
During his tenure, he reported on various topics including local crime, traffic incidents, and quality-of-life issues.

title: 12 vehicles involved in series of crashes on I-95 ramp in Danvers
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 12 vehicles were involved in a series of crashes on I-95 ramp in Danvers, caused by icy conditions.
Three adults sustained back injuries and were taken to local hospitals for evaluation, state police reported.
Massachusetts Department of Transportation treated the roadway; the ramp was reopened Wednesday morning.

title: 2 people, including child, hit by car in Dorchester
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An adult and a child were hit by a car in Dorchester Tuesday night, police reported.
The incident occurred near Mora and Washington streets at approximately 7 p.m., according to Boston Police.
Both individuals were taken to a local hospital with minor injuries; the driver stayed at the scene.
